entries.description:

From now on all Chinese traffic will be served by "near China" locations provided by global CDN providers. Dec 20, 2021 10:26 CET identified – All Quantil traffic was temporarily rerouted to Fastly. Dec 20, 2021 08:40 CET investigating – We're currently investigating an issue with one of our providers affecting traffic in mainland China.

entries.title: React Labs: What We've Been Working On – June 2022
entries.description: React 18 was years in the making, and with it brought valuable lessons for the React team. Its release was the result of many years of research and exploring many paths. entries.title: React Canaries: Enabling Incremental Feature Rollout Outside Meta
entries.description: We'd like to offer the React community an option to adopt individual new features as soon as their design is close to final, before they're released in a stable version--similar to how Meta has long used bleeding-edge versions of React internally. We are introducing a new officially supported [Canary release channel](/community/versioning-policy#canary-channel). It lets curated setups like frameworks decouple adoption of individual React features from the React release schedule.

entries.description: Tabulator turns any HTML table, JavaScript array, or JSON feed into a fully interactive data grid with sorting, filtering, inline editing, and export built in. It ships zero dependencies, an MIT license, and works with React, Vue, Angular, or plain JavaScript.

entries.title: react-inlinesvg: Give Your SVGs CSS Superpowers
entries.description: react-inlinesvg loads SVGs from URLs, paths, or raw strings and injects them inline into the DOM as real elements. That means you can style, recolor, and animate them with plain CSS, just like any other markup.

entries.title: SVGInject: Teaching Your Images to Speak CSS
entries.description: SVGInject swaps plain img tags for inline svg elements at runtime, so you can finally style every path, gradient, and group with plain CSS. No build step, no framework lock-in, and around 3.5 KB gzipped.

entries.title: STRICH: Production-Grade Barcode Scanning Without Leaving the Browser
entries.description: STRICH is a commercial JavaScript SDK that turns a device camera into a fast, reliable barcode and QR scanner running entirely in the browser. Here is how it works, how to wire it into React, and how it stacks up against the free alternatives.

entries.title: SSGOI: Give Your Web Pages the Transitions a Native App Would Brag About
entries.description: SSGOI turns plain route swaps into fluid, spring-physics page transitions that work in every browser and with any router. Here is how it works and how to wire it into a React or Next.js app.

entries.title: Shimmer From Structure: Skeletons That Measure Themselves
entries.description: shimmer-from-structure generates loading skeletons by measuring your real components at runtime, so you never hand-build a duplicate skeleton again. This guide covers static cards, dynamic templateProps, Suspense fallbacks, and global theming across React, Vue, Svelte, Angular, and SolidJS.

entries.title: Shaders: GPU Eye-Candy Without Writing a Single Line of WGSL
entries.description: Shaders is a component system for creative WebGPU effects in the browser, with wrappers for React, Vue, Svelte, Solid and vanilla JS. Drop in over a hundred GPU-powered gradients, blurs, distortions and post-effects as declarative components.

entries.title: Scroll Without End: Effortless Pagination with react-infinite-scroll-component
entries.description: react-infinite-scroll-component triggers a callback when readers approach the end of a list, so you can fetch and append the next page on demand. The v7 rewrite brings IntersectionObserver, zero dependencies, a tiny bundle, and a fresh hook API.

entries.title: Create React Native Library: Skip the Boilerplate, Ship the Module
entries.description: Create React Native Library is Callstack's CLI for scaffolding publishable React Native libraries, complete with new-architecture native modules, example apps, and build tooling. Here is how to go from an empty folder to a working Turbo Module, Fabric view, or Nitro module in minutes.

entries.title: React Color: The Variety Pack of Color Pickers Your App Has Been Missing
entries.description: @uiw/react-color is a modular, TypeScript-first collection of React color pickers, from Photoshop-style panels to curated swatch grids. Here is how to drop one in, compose your own, and theme it for dark mode.

entries.title: React-Calendar: A Calendar That Skips the Moment.js Diet
entries.description: React-Calendar is a flexible, accessible, dependency-light date and date-range picker component for React. Drop it in, restyle it, and pick days, months, years, or whole decades without dragging moment.js into your bundle.

entries.title: Nitro Player: A Batteries-Included Music Engine for React Native
entries.description: Nitro Player is a full-featured React Native audio engine built on Nitro Modules, shipping playlists, a 5-band equalizer, offline downloads, and native Android Auto and CarPlay support. This guide walks through the queue model, hooks, and equalizer that make it a modern alternative for music apps.

entries.title: Nitro Fetch: The Drop-In Fetch That Leaves React Native's Built-In One in the Dust
entries.description: Nitro Fetch is a native-powered, Web-standard drop-in replacement for React Native's fetch built on Margelo's Nitro Modules. It adds HTTP/3, prefetching, streaming, and off-thread JSON parsing while keeping the same API you already know.

entries.title: nanotags: Reactive Web Components That Skip the Shadow DOM Drama
entries.description: nanotags is a sub-3KB wrapper around native Custom Elements that adds nanostores reactivity, a fully typed builder, props, refs, and automatic cleanup. Here is how it makes light-DOM, hydration-first components pleasant to write.

entries.title: json-render Ink: When the LLM Paints Your Terminal
entries.description: json-render Ink turns guardrailed JSON specs into interactive terminal UIs built on Ink, so an AI can compose a CLI you can actually trust. Define a component catalog, let the model emit constrained JSON, and stream it straight to the console.

entries.title: Ky: Fetch With the Sharp Edges Sanded Off
entries.description: Ky is a tiny, zero-dependency HTTP client built on top of the Fetch API, adding retries, timeouts, hooks, and JSON ergonomics in about 4 KB. Here is how it turns raw fetch boilerplate into clean, composable requests.

entries.title: Ink: When Your Terminal Learns to Speak React
entries.description: Ink is a React renderer that turns components, hooks, and flexbox into interactive command-line interfaces. Learn how to build polished terminal apps with the same mental model you already use on the web.

entries.title: html-react-parser: Turn HTML Strings Into Real React Elements
entries.description: html-react-parser converts raw HTML strings into genuine React elements you can intercept, replace, and restyle. Learn how to render CMS content without reaching for dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

entries.title: Hiraki: Slide-In Drawers Without the Dependency Hangover
entries.description: Hiraki is a zero-dependency, headless React drawer primitive with velocity-aware gestures and snap points in all four directions. This early-stage library chases Vaul-class polish in about 10 KB without pulling in Radix or Framer Motion.

entries.title: Heerich.js: Stacking Voxels Into Crisp SVG, No WebGL Required
entries.description: Heerich.js is a tiny zero-dependency engine that builds 3D voxel scenes and renders them to clean SVG vectors. No canvas, no WebGL, just stackable blocks, boolean carving, and four camera projections.

entries.title: get-value: Reaching Into Nested Objects Without Faceplanting
entries.description: get-value is a tiny zero-dependency utility for reading nested object values with dot-notation paths, and it is the rare one that handles keys with dots in them. Version 4.1 adds Map-like object support on top of a TypeScript rewrite.

entries.title: Fuse.js: Fuzzy Search That Forgives Your Typos
entries.description: Fuse.js is a tiny, zero-dependency fuzzy-search library that adds typo-tolerant search to any app without a backend. Version 7.4 brings multi-word token search and parallel search across Web Workers.

entries.title: performative-ui: Components That Signal How Oversubscribed Your Round Is
entries.description: performative-ui is a satirical-but-real React component library of cliche AI-startup landing-page tropes. It ships typed, working components like ASCII heroes, node-graph backgrounds, and a Claude Code style wibbling spinner.

entries.title: Blocking Install Scripts Is Not a Silver Bullet
entries.description: npm v12 blocks install scripts by default, but supply chain attacks won't disappear. Learn why runtime execution, the Node.js permission model, and sandboxing still matter.

entries.title: pnpm 11.9
entries.description: pnpm 11.9 computes missing tarball integrity for registries that cannot publish checksums, adds pnpm sbom --exclude-peers, improves audit performance on cyclic lockfiles, fixes peer-resolution nondeterminism, and tightens exclusion handling for minimumReleaseAge and trustPolicy.

Alongside resolving upgrade regressions, we've been working on new features, and we're excited to announce the release of Vite 8.1.
